  "decision": "fail",

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Roofing nails used in joist hangers instead of approved structural connector nails",
    "Inadequate shear and withdrawal capacity due to incorrect fastener diameter and length"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove all unapproved roofing nails from the joist hangers",
    "Replace with manufacturer-specified structural connector nails (e.g., Simpson Strong-Drive SD Connector screws or 10d/16d common nails as specified by the hanger catalog)"
  ],
  "rationale": "Roofing nails (typically short, thin, and made of softer steel) do not provide the shear or withdrawal resistance required by the IRC and connector manufacturers (such as Simpson Strong-Tie) for joist hangers. This creates a high risk of structural failure under load.",
  "references": [
    "IRC Section R502.6 (Bearing)",
    "IRC Section R301.1.3 (Nominal sizes)",
    "Simpson Strong-Tie Wood Construction Connectors Catalog (Fastener Compatibility and Specifications)"
  ]
}
